title: poa pratensis var. pinegensis
intro: Poa pratensis var. pinegensis, also known as pine meadow grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Poa pratensis, native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting respiratory health. Poa pratensis var. pinegensis can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
